methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $256K total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Terran Robotics is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Zach Dwiel and Danny Weddle, having raised $256K to date. The robotics and manufacturing sector includes one public comparable (277810.KQ) and eight private peers, among them 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The stage-progression method is applicable here, using total capital raised as a value proxy and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage progression. This yields an implied valuation of $640K. The peer-median method is not applicable; while 11 same-sector peers exist, insufficient granular overlap remains when filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier comparables—a minimum of three datapoints is required for reliable triangulation, and the narrow filters produced zero matches.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $544K (low) to $736K (high), with a median of $640K. This range reflects ±15% variance around the stage-progression result given the early stage and limited peer specificity. Key uncertainties include the absence of directly comparable seed-stage robotics companies in the dataset and reliance on a single methodology. Investors should update this valuation as additional seed-stage robotics comparables emerge or as Terran approaches subsequent funding rounds with clearer operational milestones.
